behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (CSV row index -> validator -> DLQ payload), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real S3/network/OS primitives; in-process wiring can reproduce it. MOCK-HIDE: a pure unit test of validator could miss row-number propagation bugs. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior only states DLQ outcome and row number, not real HTTP/subprocess conditions; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI step is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline on a small CSV fixture with known missing required fields and assert invalid rows are written to DLQ records that include exact original row numbers while valid rows continue through normal path.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is transformation/parsing logic of one boundary; no required cross-module interaction in behavior text. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior is explicitly about conversion correctness for input formats, which can be fully exercised at function/object level.
behaviors.test_description: Call the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European date strings (including edge cases like ambiguous day/month) and assert canonical output or explicit parse failure policy.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is runtime/memory/timeout behavior that depends on real process memory and execution characteristics. MOCK-HIDE: mocks/fakes typically hide allocator pressure, GC behavior, and real timeout behavior. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because in-process mocked components cannot reliably expose OOM/timeout failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline end-to-end as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real runtime limits, asserting completion before configured timeout and memory usage staying below threshold (or no OOM/kill).

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior is tied to real HTTP/service error semantics (S3 503 transient) and retry timing behavior. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed client can miss real SDK/http-layer handling of 503 responses and retry timing interactions. Rejection-gate: Integration considered, but rejected because behavior explicitly references bucket 503 response handling, which is a real service boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline against an HTTP-level S3 test endpoint/emulator configured to return transient 503s, then success; assert retries occur with increasing backoff intervals and batch eventually succeeds without full-batch failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is state handoff across pipeline progress tracking + DB writes + restart recovery logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network condition is required beyond local DB semantics; a local PostgreSQL test DB is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: fake repositories can miss idempotency/constraint/transaction edge cases. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior does not require subprocess-level restart; restart can be simulated by re-instantiating pipeline components against persisted DB state.
behaviors.test_description: Use a local PostgreSQL test database, process part of a batch, simulate restart by creating a new pipeline instance with same checkpoint/state, resume processing, and assert no duplicate rows (via unique keys/count checks).
